title: Whole Life Insurance Is a $695K Trap
hook: Your insurance agent earned $5,700 the day you signed your whole life policy — and that money came from your future, not theirs.
angle: Whole life is sold as 'insurance plus savings' but the math shows buying term and investing the $475/month difference leaves you $695K richer — a fact agents earning 50-110% commission are incentivized to hide.
audience: Anyone in their 20s-40s who was sold whole life insurance, or who is being approached by an insurance agent right now
key_numbers: $500/month — typical whole life premium for $500K coverage
$25/month — same $500K coverage with a 20-year term policy
$695,000 — what the $475/month difference grows to in 30 years at 8%
50-110% — first-year commission agents earn on whole life policies
7 in 10 whole life policies are surrendered before ever paying out
